rulings.Case Title: GELAREH RAHBAR DDS VS. JENNIFER BATOON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Jan 12, 2011 Ruling
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 14, 2011, line 7, PLAINTIFF GELAREH RAHBAR'S Motion For Reconsideration Of January 12, 2011 Ruling. MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND TO VACATE PRIOR RULING IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER CCP 1008 OR CCP 473. PLAINTIFF DID NOT DEMONSTRATE ANY DILIGENCE, OPPOSE THE MOTION, OR CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING EVEN THOUGH PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE OF THE MOTION AND HEARING. FURTHER THERE WAS NO MISTAKE AT LAW. FILING A REPETITIVE SLAPP COMPLAINT AND THEN DISMISSING IT IN AN UNTIMELY FASHION IS NOT A REASONABLE MISTAKE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, VS. RICCI CORNELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff City And County Of San Francisco, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 14, 2011, line 18, Plaintiff City And County Of San Francisco, Motion For Summary Adjudication. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENSES 3A AND 3B (HABITABILITY/REPAIRS) AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS AS THESE DEFENSES ARE ONLY ALLOWED IN RESIDENTIAL NON-PAYMENT CASES. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO DISCRIMINATION DEFENSE AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SUCCESSFULLY ARGUES THAT DEFENDANT'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES ARE CONCLUSORY NOT FACTUAL AND PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT IT RECENTLY EVICTED MALE TENANTS TO CONTRADICT DEFENDANT'S CONCLUSION. THIS SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO DEFENDANT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT, WHICH WAS NOT DONE. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS TO THE RETALIATION DEFENSE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. FURTHER, DEFENDANT COMPLAINT RE: SEWAGE LEAKING INTO THE BAY RAISES A PUBLIC ISSUE AND THE DEFENSE IS PROPER UNDER CUSTOM PARKING, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1982) 138 CAL.APP.3RD 90. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ALL OF CORZAC'S DEFENSES. CHARTER SECTION 6.102 MERELY STATES THAT THE CITY ATTORNEY MUST APPROVE AS TO FORM ALL CONTRACTS. SECOND, PLAINTIFF RELIES ON PARAGRAPHS 6 & 7 OF REYNOLDS DECLARATION TO ARGUE CORZAC DID NOT MEET REQUIRED CONDITIONS. THE DECLARATION IS TOTALLY CONCLUSORY. THIRD, DEFENDANT PROPERLY CITES LENTZ AND HAS A STRONG ESTOPPEL ARGUMENT. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF AND CORZAC ACTED LIKE THE LEASE WAS PROPERLY EXECUTED FOR YEARS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. =(302/LMG)
